observation.images.image.comment: Primary slot, image0 (CALVIN rgb_static). The checkpoint's XVLAProcessor (CLIPImageProcessor, size 224) accepts any raw RGB view: bicubic resize to 224x224, rescale 1/255, ImageNet mean/std, CHW. A third (empty) view slot is zero-padded and masked.

observation.images.wrist_image.comment: Wrist slot, image1 (CALVIN rgb_gripper, 84x84 upscaled by the processor).

observation.text.comment: Task instruction (language conditioning); BART tokenizer, max length 50.

observation.state.tcp_pos.comment: TCP position in world frame. Initialized from robot_obs[0:3] at episode start; on subsequent inferences the official client feeds back the last executed action's arm0 target instead of the env state.

observation.state.tcp_rot6d.comment: TCP orientation as 6D rotation from robot_obs euler XYZ. Layout is X-VLA's interleaved-column convention: as_matrix()[:, :2].reshape(6) = [r00, r01, r10, r11, r20, r21] (NOT two stacked columns).

observation.state.gripper.comment: Binary open indicator (robot_obs[14] > 0). Effectively unused: the ee6d action space zeroes gripper channels (indices 9, 19) in proprio before the forward pass.

observation.state.arm1_padding.comment: Second-arm slot of the unified 20-dim ee6d proprio, zero-padded (CALVIN is single-arm). The model additionally right-pads the state to max_state_dim internally.

action.rel_tcp_pos.comment: CALVIN rel_actions translation, produced by the agent-side calvin_rel adapter: clip((target_pos - tcp_pos) * 50, -1, 1) from the native absolute arm0 target.

action.rel_tcp_euler.comment: CALVIN rel_actions rotation: clip(wrap(target_euler - tcp_euler) * 20, -1, 1), euler XYZ in world frame (target_euler from the native rot6d).

action.gripper.comment: Binary gripper from the native sigmoid channel, official threshold 0.8: open(+1) if raw < 0.8 else close(-1). Absolute even though the arm is delta.

comment: X-VLA 0.9B finetuned for CALVIN ABC->D (Franka on PlayTable, 30 Hz), checkpoint 2toINF/X-VLA-Calvin-ABC_D (transformers remote-code XVLA class + XVLAProcessor; no lerobot-format CALVIN checkpoint exists). Soft-prompt domain_id 2, flow-matching with 10 denoising steps, num_actions=30 per inference (chunk_size); the official eval client executes only the first 20 actions of each chunk before re-inferring. The native output is the unified 20-dim two-arm ee6d space (per arm: pos3 + interleaved rot6d6 + sigmoid gripper1); the agent-side calvin_rel adapter (demos/benchmarks/agents/xvla_calvin.py) reduces arm0 to the CALVIN 7-dim rel_actions specced here, pairing with the env's default 'relative' branch. (The official client instead drives calvin_env with the absolute (pos, quat, gripper) tuple — the env's 'absolute' branch supports that form too; the rel reduction re-derives the delta against the live tcp pose each step, an approximation flagged OPEN below.) OPEN: rel reduction vs official absolute control may differ slightly when the model's target exceeds the per-step rel clip (0.02 m / 0.05 rad) or when calvin_env's integrated internal target pose drifts from the observed tcp pose. OPEN: official score 4.43/5 was measured with EP_LEN=720 per subtask (the standard CALVIN harness uses 360). Full 20-dim native form lives in contracts/experiments/xvla.json.
